rationale_for_baseline_tier: An acutely infected venous ulcer (peri-wound cellulitis) needs prompt same-day in-person assessment/wound care and antibiotics, but without systemic sepsis — T3, escalating to T4 for spreading cellulitis/sepsis.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Peri-wound cellulitis (new tender warm spreading redness) on a venous ulcer
escalation_edges.rationale: Secondary cellulitis needs prompt antibiotics and wound care.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Your ulcer has a skin infection — you need same-day review for antibiotics and a proper dressing, and keep the leg raised.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Rapidly ascending calf redness, whole-leg swelling/severe pain, or high fever/rigors
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness races up your calf, your whole leg swells and is severely painful, or you get a high fever or shivering — go to the emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Arrange community wound-care support and ensure she can attend review and elevate the leg.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Your ulcer has a skin infection. Start the antibiotics, keep the leg raised above heart level, keep the dressing dry, and get same-day review and a wound-clinic referral. Don't put the tight stockings back on until the infection settles and your circulation is checked. Watch for redness climbing the calf or a fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day GP/wound clinic + antibiotics; ED for spreading cellulitis/sepsis
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: After-hours GP for antibiotics/dressing; ED for ascending cellulitis or fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document an infected venous leg ulcer (secondary cellulitis; sepsis/deep infection screened negative), antibiotics + elevation + absorbent dressing, ABPI and compression plan deferred, and urgent wound-clinic referral.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Routine dressing advice with no antibiotics/elevation or sepsis safety-net (or resuming compression during infection).
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: The cellulitis spreads to sepsis, or premature compression on an arterial-compromised limb causes harm.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Deferring to a routine appointment without same-day antibiotics/wound care for an infected ulcer.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance for a localised peri-wound cellulitis without systemic toxicity.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot palpate pulses/ABPI or assess wound depth remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the cellulitis with antibiotics + elevation + wound-clinic referral, defer compression until infection settles and ABPI is done, and safety-net for sepsis/deep infection.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Advising compression now, or dependency (arterial advice), or missing ascending cellulitis.
